stemHtml: When analysing a new route that transits over high terrain, it is determined that the twin-engined Commercial Air Transport Performance Class A jet aeroplane is NOT able to maintain a positive climb gradient 1000 feet above the highest terrain on the route with one-engine inoperative. To be able to perform the route, the operator must therefore develop a suitable drift-down procedure. During this, the minimum obstacle-clearance height above all terrain and obstructions to be considered is…
